title: Constrain IAM policies with explicit resources and condition keys
description: Scope every statement to named resource ARNs and add condition keys (aws:SourceArn, aws:PrincipalTag, StringEquals) instead of granting Action or Resource wildcards.
body: Use when authoring or reviewing an IAM identity/resource policy or trust relationship. Do not ship Action:* or Resource:* to make a call succeed, and avoid wildcard service prefixes like s3:* when only GetObject is needed. Actions: enumerate the exact API actions the workload calls, pin Resource to specific ARNs, add condition keys (aws:SourceArn for service principals, StringEquals on aws:PrincipalTag, IpAddress on aws:SourceIp) and use iam simulate-principal-policy to confirm out-of-scope actions are denied. Validation signals include the policy simulator returning Deny for unlisted actions and CloudTrail showing no AccessDenied for the intended path. Risk boundary: any statement that widens a principal's reach or adds a wildcard is human-gated; escalate rather than broaden.
